title: PostgreSQL table engine ignores time zone offset when reading timestamptz into DateTime
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
The PostgreSQL table engine reads `timestamptz`/`timestamp with time zone` columns into `DateTime` (with or without an explicit time zone) using the Postgres server's time zone, not the time zone of the `DateTime` column (which defaults to the ClickHouse server's time zone).
Internally, it appears that [the output of `COPY (SELECT ...) TO STDOUT`](https://clickhouse.com/docs/engines/table-engines/integrations/postgresql#implementation-details) is being parsed with the offset being ignored, e.g. a `timestamptz` outputted as `1999-12-31 19:00:00-05` is parsed as `1999-12-31 19:00:00` without the `-05` offset.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
I have a table in a PostgreSQL server in the US/Central time zone with a `timestamptz` (aka `timestamp with time zone`) column:
```sql
CREATE TABLE my_pg_table (
stamp timestamptz PRIMARY KEY
);
INSERT INTO my_pg_table VALUES ('2000-01-01T00:00:00Z');
SHOW timezone;
-- US/Central
```
(The column output is unambiguous because the date/time string always contains a time zone offset, e.g. `1999-12-31 19:00:00.000 -0500`.)
I create a remote connection to that table in ClickHouse:
```sql
CREATE TABLE ch_pg_table_inferred
ENGINE = PostgreSQL('example.com:5432', 'my_database_name', 'my_pg_table', 'my_username', 'my_password');
```
ClickHouse settings:
- `date_time_input_format`: `best_effort`
- `date_time_output_format`: `iso`
- `SELECT timezone()`: `UTC`
When I read the timestamp through ClickHouse, the `DateTime` column contains the date/time in the PostgreSQL server's time zone instead of the ClickHouse server's time zone (UTC):
```sql
SELECT stamp FROM ch_pg_table_inferred;
-- 1999-12-31 19:00:00
```
Even if I create the table with an explicit `DateTime('UTC')` column, ClickHouse still doesn't take into account the offset returned by PostgreSQL:
```sql
CREATE TABLE ch_pg_table_utc (
stamp DateTime('UTC')
)
ENGINE = PostgreSQL('example.com:5432', 'my_database_name', 'my_pg_table', 'my_username', 'my_password');
SELECT stamp FROM ch_pg_table_utc;
-- 1999-12-31 19:00:00.000 +0000
```
But If I instead create the remote connection with a `String` column, I can recover the real timestamp with `parseDateTimeBestEffort`:
```sql
CREATE TABLE ch_pg_table_string (
stamp String
)
ENGINE = PostgreSQL('example.com:5432', 'my_database_name', 'my_pg_table', 'my_username', 'my_password');
SELECT
stamp,
parseDateTimeBestEffort(stamp) AS stamp_default,
key_bug_features: PostgreSQL engine ignores time zone offset for timestamptz
bug_features_seed: SELECT stamp, parseDateTimeBestEffort(stamp) AS parsed_stamp FROM ch_pg_table_string;
